answers.field_label: Describe Current Uses Of The Property
answers.value:
The subject property at 1845 Lakeside Drive is currently improved with a 2½-story masonry duplex originally constructed in 1928, containing two legal dwelling units and a detached two-car garage accessed from the rear alley. The building has been used continuously as a two-family residence since at least the early 1960s, based on prior title records and conversations with long-time neighbors.

The first-floor unit (Unit 1) is approximately 1,250 square feet and is presently leased to a young family (two adults and one child). It consists of two bedrooms, one bathroom, a combined living/dining room, and a kitchen that was modestly updated in 2014 (new cabinets, countertops, and appliances). The tenants also have non-exclusive use of the shared front porch, rear deck, and basement laundry area.

The second-floor unit (Unit 2) is approximately 1,150 square feet and is currently occupied by my retired aunt, who has lived in this unit for the past eleven years. It has a similar layout to the first-floor unit (two bedrooms, one bathroom, living room, dining room, and kitchen) but with slightly smaller bedroom sizes due to the roofline. This unit also has access to an enclosed three-season porch at the rear that is used for storage and as a small reading area.

The basement is unfinished and used primarily for shared storage and building mechanicals (boiler, hot water heaters, and electrical panels). There is a small, non-habitable laundry area with two washers and two dryers for the exclusive use of the residents of the two legal units. No additional dwelling unit, short-term rental, or commercial use is established in the basement.

The detached two-car garage at the rear of the lot is used for parking by the first-floor tenants (one space) and by me, the owner (one space) when I visit the property for maintenance, inspections, and family visits. There are also two legal off-street parking spaces in front of the garage apron that are used intermittently for guest parking.

No portion of the property is used for commercial, industrial, institutional, or short-term rental purposes. The property is not advertised on any short-term rental platforms, and all leases are for a minimum term of 12 months. There are no home occupations or business signage on site. Outdoor areas are used only for typical residential purposes such as gardening, grilling, and children’s play. The existing use is therefore a straightforward, long-standing two-family residential use consistent with the R3 – Two-Family Residential District.
